title: Filthy Midnight Filipino Pork Menudo
description: A rich, savory, and spicy Filipino menudo dish perfect for a midnight craving. This recipe features a unique combination of ingredients and cooking techniques.

ingredients.name: Pork belly, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Use a meat cleaver to cut the pork belly into uniform pieces

ingredients.name: Onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use a mandoline to get thin, uniform chops

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Use a microplane to get fine, even minces

ingredients.name: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality fish sauce for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Patis (Filipino fish sauce)
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality patis for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Vinegar
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality vinegar for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Egg rolls, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use leftover egg rolls or purchase them from an Asian market

ingredients.name: Manteca (rendered pork fat)
ingredients.notes: Use homemade or store-bought manteca for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Chili peppers, sliced
ingredients.notes: Use fresh or dried chili peppers for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Bay leaves
ingredients.notes: Use fresh or dried bay leaves for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Tomato sauce
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality tomato sauce for the best flavor

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of manteca in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the fish sauce, patis, vinegar, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the pork belly and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped egg rolls, chili peppers, and bay leaves. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add the tomato sauce and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the pork is tender.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro or scallions if desired.
